file.content:
{
  "name": "personalwebsite",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "private": true,
  "packageManager": "npm@10.9.2",
  "engines": {
    "node": ">=20"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "next dev",
    "build": "next build",
    "postbuild": "next-sitemap --config next-sitemap.config.js && npm run build:resume-pdf",
    "build:resume-pdf": "tsx scripts/resume/build-pdf.ts",
    "start": "next start",
    "lint": "next lint",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "test": "vitest run",
    "test:e2e": "playwright test",
    "visual": "playwright test e2e/visual.spec.ts",
    "visual:update": "playwright test e2e/visual.spec.ts --update-snapshots",
    "lighthouse:ci": "lhci autorun --config=./.lighthouserc.json",
    "ship-gate:projects": "tsx scripts/ship-gates/projects.ts",
    "smoke": "tsx scripts/smoke/run-against.ts",
    "ingest": "tsx scripts/llm/ingest.ts",
    "ingest:rebuild": "tsx scripts/llm/ingest.ts --rebuild",
    "evals:llm": "tsx evals/llm/run.ts",
    "db:generate": "drizzle-kit generate",
    "db:migrate": "tsx scripts/llm/migrate.ts"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@anthropic-ai/sdk": "^0.91.1",
    "@mdx-js/loader": "^3.1.1",
    "@mdx-js/react": "^3.1.1",
    "@next/mdx": "^16.2.4",
    "@radix-ui/react-dialog": "^1.1.15",
    "@radix-ui/react-visually-hidden": "^1.2.4",
    "@types/mdx": "^2.0.13",
    "@upstash/redis": "^1.37.0",
    "@vercel/analytics": "^2.0.1",
    "drizzle-orm": "^0.45.2",
    "framer-motion": "^12.38.0",
    "fuse.js": "^7.3.0",
    "next": "^15.1.0",
    "next-sitemap": "^4.2.3",
    "pgvector": "^0.2.1",
    "postgres": "^3.4.9",
    "react": "^19.0.0",
    "react-dom": "^19.0.0",
    "zod": "^4.3.6",
    "zustand": "^5.0.12"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@anthropic-ai/tokenizer": "^0.0.4",
    "@axe-core/playwright": "^4.11.2",
    "@axe-core/react": "^4.11.2",
    "@lhci/cli": "^0.14.0",
    "@next/bundle-analyzer": "^16.2.4",
    "@playwright/test": "^1.59.1",
    "@testing-library/react": "^16.3.2",
    "@types/node": "^22.0.0",
    "@types/react": "^19.0.0",
    "@types/react-dom": "^19.0.0",
    "axe-core": "^4.11.3",
    "drizzle-kit": "^0.31.10",
    "eslint": "^9.0.0",
    "eslint-config-next": "^15.1.0",
    "jsdom": "^29.0.2",
    "prettier": "^3.8.3",
    "stylelint": "^17.8.0",
    "stylelint-config-standard": "^40.0.0",
    "tsx": "^4.21.0",
    "typescript": "^5.0.0",
    "vitest": "^4.1.4"
  }
}

typography.css
---
/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 * tokens.css — operator's portfolio
 * Canonical custom properties. Consumed by every component.
 * Dark is canonical; light is opt-in (toggle implemented separately).
 *
 * Vertical rhythm rule (suite 13/P03):
 *   All vertical spacing between content blocks is a multiple of
 *   var(--s-2) (8px). Text within a block follows the line-height
 *   of its type role. This rule is for prose; UI elements may use
 *   finer increments via the --s-* scale.
 * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- */

:root,
:root[data-theme="dark"] {
  /* ----- v2 semantic colour palette (suite 13/P02) -----
     Dark is canonical. Hex with alpha (#RRGGBBAA) keeps tokens
     self-contained — no rgba(). All fg/bg pairs documented in
     docs/v2_audit.md ## Contrast deviations meet WCAG AA. */

  /* surface */
  --bg:           #0c0e10;            /* base background          */
  --bg-mute:      #14171a;            /* muted surface            */
  --bg-raise:     #1b1f23;            /* raised surface (cards)   */
  --bg-overlay:   #06070824;          /* modal overlay            */

  /* foreground */
  --fg:           #e8e6e1;            /* primary text             */
  --fg-mute:      #8b8a85;            /* secondary text           */
  --fg-faint:     #9a9994;            /* tertiary text — adjusted to clear AA-normal (4.5:1) on --bg and --bg-mute */
  --fg-inverse:   #0c0e10;            /* text on accent surface   */

  /* borders + dividers */
  --line:         #232629;            /* hairline                 */
  --line-strong:  #3a3d40;            /* emphatic border          */

  /* accent — warm gold/amber inherited from v1 (B3). Do not change without authorization. */
  --accent:       #c9a96e;            /* primary accent (mono)    */
  --accent-mute:  #c9a96e22;          /* hover/wash               */
  --accent-fg:    #0c0e10;            /* text on accent           */

  /* status — used sparingly (chat error states only) */
  --warn:         #d18b3a;
  --error:        #dc6e69;            /* adjusted from #d2615c to clear AA on bg-raise (see audit) */
  --ok:           #6da16a;

  /* ----- v1 aliases (transitional) -------------------------
     Active components in src/components/* still reference v1
     names. Aliases map them onto the v2 palette so the visual
     ramp degrades gracefully until each component is migrated
     in subsequent suite-13 prompts. New code MUST consume the

     Dark is canonical. Hex with alpha (#RRGGBBAA) keeps tokens
     self-contained — no rgba(). All fg/bg pairs documented in
     docs/v2_audit.md ## Contrast deviations meet WCAG AA. */

  /* surface */
  --bg:           #0c0e10;            /* base background          */
  --bg-mute:      #14171a;            /* muted surface            */
  --bg-raise:     #1b1f23;            /* raised surface (cards)   */
  --bg-overlay:   #06070824;          /* modal overlay            */

  /* foreground */
  --fg:           #e8e6e1;            /* primary text             */
  --fg-mute:      #8b8a85;            /* secondary text           */
  --fg-faint:     #9a9994;            /* tertiary text — adjusted to clear AA-normal (4.5:1) on --bg and --bg-mute */
  --fg-inverse:   #0c0e10;            /* text on accent surface   */

  /* borders + dividers */
  --line:         #232629;            /* hairline                 */
  --line-strong:  #3a3d40;            /* emphatic border          */

  /* accent — warm gold/amber inherited from v1 (B3). Do not change without authorization. */
  --accent:       #c9a96e;            /* primary accent (mono)    */
  --accent-mute:  #c9a96e22;          /* hover/wash               */
  --accent-fg:    #0c0e10;            /* text on accent           */

  /* status — used sparingly (chat error states only) */
  --warn:         #d18b3a;
  --error:        #dc6e69;            /* adjusted from #d2615c to clear AA on bg-raise (see audit) */
  --ok:           #6da16a;

  /* ----- v1 aliases (transitional) -------------------------
     Active components in src/components/* still reference v1
     names. Aliases map them onto the v2 palette so the visual
     ramp degrades gracefully until each component is migrated
     in subsequent suite-13 prompts. New code MUST consume the

input.content:
# v2 Ship Checklist (suite 22/P05)

> **The user's verbatim diagnosis:** "completely unusable."
>
> The v2 rebuild was scoped against that single sentence. The ship gate
> is therefore a definitional gate, not just a numeric one: v2 ships
> when this complaint is no longer defensible by an honest reading of
> the deployed site. If a fair, unfriendly visitor could still type
> "completely unusable" without obvious bad faith, **stop and fix —
> regardless of what `npm run ship-gate` reports.**

## How to use this checklist

1. Run `npm run ship-gate`. It must exit 0. (That covers the
   programmatic half — typecheck, build, primitive tests, e2e, ingest
   dry-run, microcopy lint, Lighthouse, axe, smoke, visual.)
2. Walk through every box below in order. The checklist is the human
   half — what scripts cannot verify.
3. Sign off at the bottom. **Do not auto-ship on a green ship-gate.**

---

## Manual review

### Content read-through

- [ ] Read every project page top to bottom. (Hivemind, Purposeless
      Efficiency, Theseus — every section, not just the hero.)
- [ ] Click through every presentation deck — every slide, forward
      and back, including any deep-linked slide URLs.
- [ ] Skim `/about`, `/resume`, the changelog, and the styleguide for
      stale copy or v1 ghosts.

### Chat (`/chat`)

- [ ] Send 5 different chat questions covering: a project detail, a
      resume fact, a philosophical/voice question, a vague open-ended
      question, and a question that requires multi-source synthesis.
      Verify citations appear on grounded answers.
- [ ] Send an out-of-scope question (e.g. "what's the weather in
      Berlin?"). Verify graceful refusal in voice — no apology
      cascade, no hallucinated answer.
- [ ] Send an empty submit, a 5,000-character submit, and a submit
      with markdown / code fences. Nothing breaks.
- [ ] Trigger an error path (e.g. unplug network mid-stream). The
      retry affordance shows and works.

### Theme + responsiveness

- [ ] Switch to light theme on every page; nothing breaks. Contrast
      still passes; no dark-mode-only colours leak through.
- [ ] Resize from 320 px to 1920 px on the landing, projects index,
      a project detail, `/chat`, and a presentation. No horizontal
      scroll, no clipped focus rings.

### Keyboard + a11y

- [ ] Use only the keyboard for 5 minutes across landing → projects
      → project detail → chat → presentation. No focus traps. Skip-
      link works on every page. Command palette opens on `cmd-k` /
      `ctrl-k` and is fully operable without a mouse.
- [ ] Run a screen reader (VoiceOver / NVDA) on `/`, `/projects`,
      `/chat`. Headings announce in order; live regions speak chat
      streams; nothing reads "button button button".

### Real-device sanity

- [ ] Open on a real iPhone: chat works, mobile drawer opens and
      closes, fonts render (no FOUT into Times New Roman), the
      keyboard does not cover the chat input, hit targets are large
      enough.
- [ ] Open on a real Android phone (Chrome). Same checks.
- [ ] Open in Safari desktop and Firefox desktop — the site is not
      Chrome-only.

### Social + share surfaces

- [ ] Share landing on Twitter / Slack / iMessage; OG card looks
      intentional (right title, right image, right description).
      Repeat for `/chat`, a project detail, a presentation deck.
- [ ] View source on `/`: title, description, canonical, OG, Twitter
      card meta are all present and correct.

### Performance — real-world

- [ ] Lighthouse mobile run on each route from a fresh tab (not just
      CI). Performance ≥ target on a real network throttled to Slow
      4G. Largest Contentful Paint feels snappy, not just measured.
- [ ] Cold-load the site on a phone over cellular. The first
      meaningful paint isn't a blank dark rectangle for >1 s.

### Voice + copy drift

- [ ] Open `docs/voice-guide.md` next to three random pages of copy.
      Flag any drift: forbidden words, mixed casing in chrome,
      exclamation marks outside the Unix-error convention, anything
      that would feel like marketing fluff to a hostile reader.
- [ ] All UI strings live in `src/content/microcopy.ts`. Spot-check
      by grepping `src/components/**/*.tsx` for string literals that
      look like user-facing copy and aren't pulled from microcopy.

### Final passes

- [ ] Open every page once with DevTools console visible. No errors,
      no warnings about missing keys, no hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Open the deployed URL in an incognito window. No leftover
      auth, no cached state. The first impression is the test.
- [ ] Re-read this checklist. Anything you skipped because "it's
      probably fine" — go check it.

---

## Sign-off

- [ ] User signed off.

content.content: File created successfully at: /Users/michaelquintin/Desktop/Main Desktop - Michael's MacBook Pro/Projects/Personal_Website/docs/v2_ship_checklist.md (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

content.content:
# v2 Ship Checklist (suite 22/P05)

> **The user's verbatim diagnosis:** "completely unusable."
>
> The v2 rebuild was scoped against that single sentence. The ship gate
> is therefore a definitional gate, not just a numeric one: v2 ships
> when this complaint is no longer defensible by an honest reading of
> the deployed site. If a fair, unfriendly visitor could still type
> "completely unusable" without obvious bad faith, **stop and fix —
> regardless of what `npm run ship-gate` reports.**

## How to use this checklist

1. Run `npm run ship-gate`. It must exit 0. (That covers the
   programmatic half — typecheck, build, primitive tests, e2e, ingest

content.text:
## Honest report

**What ran:**
- `npx tsc --noEmit` (full project typecheck): clean — orchestrator compiles under the repo's tsconfig.
- Inline validation of the orchestrator's two pure checks against current repo state:
  - `checkTokensCss` — all required tokens present in `src/styles/tokens.css`.
  - `checkSitemapAndRobots` — `public/robots.txt` and `public/sitemap.xml` both exist.
- `npm run` registry confirms `ship-gate` and `ship-gate:projects` are both wired.
- `docs/v2_ship_checklist.md` exists and opens with the user's verbatim "completely unusable" diagnosis as required by §B3.

**What I did NOT run end-to-end:** the full `npm run ship-gate`. It would invoke `npm run build`, Lighthouse CI, Playwright (chat/landing/presentations/a11y/visual/smoke), an ingest dry-run, and a smoke test against `http://localhost:3000` — that requires a running dev server, the chat DB, and the Anthropic key, plus 5–15 minutes of runtime. Per the prompt's honesty contract, the orchestrator deliberately does not silently skip those; it fails loudly when the environment is not set up.

**Per-gate plan timing (definitions, not measurements):** Gate 12 (~typecheck+build, ~60–120s) → 13 (vitest primitives, ~5–10s) → 14 (HTTP probe of 6 shell paths, ~1s) → 15/17/19 (Playwright suites, ~30–90s each) → 16 (delegates to existing `ship-gate:projects`, ~120s) → 18 (ingest dry-run + chat route vitest, ~10–30s) → 20 (microcopy lint vitest, ~3s) → 21 (Lighthouse CI + axe + sitemap check, ~120–300s) → 22 (smoke + visual playwright, ~60–120s).

**Files written:**
- `scripts/ship-gates/all.ts` — orchestrator (gates 12–22, per-gate steps, summary).
- `package.json` — added `"ship-gate": "tsx scripts/ship-gates/all.ts"`.
- `docs/v2_ship_checklist.md` — manual checklist with all required boxes plus the "User signed off" sign-off line.

IMPL_RESULT: PASS
